skill.name: hypogenic
skill.description: Automated LLM-driven hypothesis generation and testing for tabular datasets; use when you need systematic exploration of empirical patterns (e.g., fraud detection, content analysis) and want to combine literature insights with data-driven hypothesis evaluation.
skill.summary: Automated LLM-driven hypothesis generation and testing for tabular datasets; use when you need systematic exploration of empirical patterns (e.g., fraud detection, content analysis) and want to combine literature insights with data-driven hypothesis evaluation.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 57 static findings were evaluated. The vast majority are FALSE POSITIVES: weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance detections are text in an audit JSON file; shell backtick detections are markdown code block examples; C2 keyword detection matches a legitimate academic tool (s2orc-doc2json). Two TRUE POSITIVE findings are benign: a config template documents OPENAI_API_KEY usage (not a real key), and a hardcoded URL links to the project repository. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected. The skill is legitimate research tooling documentation.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detections in audit result JSON
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hypogenic_audit_result_v1.json file contains only text descriptions, evaluation metadata, and legacy review notes. No cryptographic code exists in this file. The static analyzer matched on JSON string values containing incidental text patterns.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detections in audit result JSON
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Lines matched contain evaluation commentary text such as 'not see this scenario'. There is no system reconnaissance behavior. This is a pattern-based false match on natural language in an evaluation report.

low_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: All matches are inside markdown fenced code blocks showing example shell commands and Python code for documentation purposes. No actual shell execution occurs. The SKILL.md is a documentation file, not an executable script.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detections in config template YAML
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Matches are in prompt text templates and configuration field descriptions within config_template.yaml. These are example prompt strings and task description fields, not cryptographic code or algorithms.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection in SKILL.md frontmatter
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The match is on the YAML frontmatter description field of SKILL.md which describes the skill's purpose. No cryptographic content is present.

low_findings.title: C2 keywords detection in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The term 's2orc-doc2json' is a legitimate academic paper processing tool from the Semantic Scholar S2ORC project. The static analyzer misidentified the 's2' prefix as a C2 (command and control) indicator.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE but BENIGN: The URL links to the skill's source repository on GitHub (aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a legitimate source attribution link, not malicious network activity.

low_findings.title: Generic API/secret keys in config template YAML
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE but BENIGN: The config_template.yaml documents how users should configure their environment variable for API key access. It contains a field name 'api_key_env' and a placeholder value 'OPENAI_API_KEY'. This is template documentation, not a real credential or exfiltration mechanism.

dangerous_patterns.title: API key environment variable reference in config template
dangerous_patterns.description: The config template references OPENAI_API_KEY as an environment variable. This is legitimate documentation informing users how to configure API access for the hypogenic library, but the pattern is logged as it represents a potential risk if the skill were modified to exfiltrate credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command documentation in markdown code blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains example bash commands for installing and running the hypogenic library in markdown fenced code blocks. These are documentation examples and not executable within the skill context, but the pattern is logged as shell execution capability.

content.user_title: Generate and test research hypotheses with AI
content.value_statement: Manual hypothesis generation is slow and may miss important patterns in data. This skill automates the process by combining literature insights with systematic data analysis.
actual_capabilities: Generates testable, falsifiable hypotheses from tabular datasets using LLM feedback
Integrates literature insights from research papers with empirical data analysis
Evaluates hypotheses consistently on validation and test data splits
Merges and deduplicates hypotheses from multiple generation sources
Supports custom prompt templates with dataset field variable injection
Enables Redis caching for cost-efficient iterative hypothesis refinement
limitations: Requires an external LLM API key with sufficient quota for multi-step generation
Hypothesis quality depends on clarity and relevance of dataset features
Literature workflows require external PDF processing services such as GROBID
Performance on stress and boundary scenarios may show weaker consistency

use_cases.title: Exploratory hypothesis discovery for data scientists
use_cases.description: When you receive a new tabular dataset, use this skill to automatically generate testable hypotheses from observed patterns and rank them by predictive value.

use_cases.title: Literature-informed empirical research
use_cases.description: Extract claims from research papers and test them against real data to discover novel empirical patterns that pure data analysis would miss.

use_cases.title: Systematic fraud pattern analysis
use_cases.description: Generate and benchmark competing hypotheses for fraud detection tasks using structured evaluation on validation and test splits.

prompt_templates.title: Simple hypothesis generation from CSV data
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a dataset stored at data/my_data.csv with columns: text, label. I want to generate hypotheses about what patterns distinguish the classes. Please help me set up a basic hypogenic configuration and run hypothesis generation.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a simple binary classification dataset

prompt_templates.title: Hypothesis generation with paper integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I have research papers in PDF format in the papers/ directory and a dataset at data/train.json. I want to extract claims from these papers and refine them against my data using the HypoRefine method.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher who wants to combine literature review with data analysis

prompt_templates.title: Custom prompt templates for domain-specific analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to customize the hypothesis generation prompts for my medical diagnosis dataset. Help me edit the prompt templates in my config.yaml file to include domain-specific constraints and output format requirements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Domain expert needing tailored hypothesis generation prompts

prompt_templates.title: End-to-end hypothesis testing pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a complete hypogenic pipeline for my dataset. Configure generation of 50 initial hypotheses, evaluate them on a held-out test set, rank by performance metrics, and output the top 10 most predictive hypotheses with their accuracy, precision, and recall scores.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running a full experimental workflow

output_examples.input: Help me generate hypotheses from my customer churn dataset
output: Generated 20 testable hypotheses based on customer behavior patterns in your dataset
Top hypothesis: Customers with more than 3 support tickets in the first month are 4 times more likely to churn (accuracy: 82 percent)
All hypotheses have been saved to ./output/hypotheses.json for further analysis

output_examples.input: I want to combine findings from 5 research papers with my experimental data
output: Extracted 15 claims from 5 papers in the literature directory
Merged 8 literature-derived hypotheses with 20 data-driven hypotheses using the Union method
Refined bank contains 22 unique hypotheses after deduplication

best_practices: Provide balanced class examples in training data to prevent biased hypothesis generation
Use validation splits for iterative refinement and keep test data separate for final evaluation
Enable Redis caching to reduce API costs during multi-iteration hypothesis optimization
anti_patterns: Do not use overlapping features between training and test splits, as this inflates performance metrics
Avoid generating too many hypotheses in a single batch, which reduces quality and increases cost
Do not skip label extraction customization, as default patterns may not match your output format

faq.question: What type of datasets does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with tabular datasets in JSON format with text features and categorical labels.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the hypogenic library requires an LLM API key such as OpenAI or Anthropic.

faq.question: Can I use my own custom prompt templates?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure custom prompt templates in the YAML config file for observations, generation, and inference.

faq.question: What is the difference between HypoGeniC and HypoRefine methods?
faq.answer: HypoGeniC uses only your dataset for hypothesis generation, while HypoRefine also integrates literature from research papers.

faq.question: How long does hypothesis generation typically take?
faq.answer: Generation time depends on dataset size, number of hypotheses, and API latency. Redis caching can reduce repeated calls.

faq.question: Can this skill be used for clinical or diagnostic purposes?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed for research and planning purposes only, not for clinical or diagnostic advice.
